directive_consumed.title: Author PROP-069 — recalibrate PROP-014 Mechanism 3 narrative-cite audit

modes_checked.mode_2_infrastructure_fuse: partial: disk-pressure observed (94-97% root FS used pre/post-clone; not exceeding empowerment thresholds because the only /tmp/*-clone is this run's own tinker-clone, ~288 MB). End-of-run cleanup will reclaim. No stale clones >24h to sweep.
modes_checked.mode_4_proposals_and_self_fixes: this run executed Mode 4 to author PROP-069

findings.description: Authored PROP-069-narrative-cite-recalibration.json per DIRECTIVE-20260531-006 (operator filed medium 2026-05-31T08:30Z). The PROP recalibrates PROP-014 Mechanism 3 in three independent ways: (1) Stage 1 targeting filter drops daily-report.pipeline_status and daily-report.recommended_actions[].action surfaces entirely (purely operational/imperative by design), applies claim-shape paragraph filter to tinker.report.findings[].description, keeps curmudgeon.reviews.* unchanged (claim-dense by design). (2) Stage 2 CITATION_RE hardening: path-start-only matching to suppress ~42% of file-missing that are prose-with-trailing-filename mis-captures, plus a well-known-bare-filename auto-resolution table to recover another ~23% of file-missing (e.g., '(open-issues.json)' auto-resolves to 'monitor/decisions/open-issues.json'). (3) Threshold recalibration from 0% to <=15% claim-uncited; new citation-resolve-rate metric targeted at >=85%; flat caps of <=20 on bogus-anchor and file-missing. Projected impact based on 3,445-paragraph sample evaluation: 3,668 -> ~1,640 claim-uncited (55% reduction). The remaining 1,640 is the genuine claim-backing gap — 545/640 = 85% of curmudgeon review files have zero citations anywhere. The recalibration reveals rather than fixes this; a follow-up behavioral PROP for curmudgeon citation discipline is the next step (out of scope).

During the PROP-069 file-missing investigation, the dominant defect class (42% of 66 sampled file-missing) is malformed_runon: prose containing a side-mention of a JSON filename gets captured by the current over-permissive CITATION_RE pattern as a citation. Example: 'WIN-002.c4, WIN-007.c3, WIN-060.c5 — all minor severity, all verdict_holds=true, recommended_action=no_change per pending-digest.json:pending_reviews' is parsed as a citation with file path equal to the entire prose-up-to-and-including-the-filename. The regex hardening proposed in PROP-069 fixes this at the source. Secondary effect: ~10-20 of the current 80 bogus-anchor citations are similarly prose-as-citation captures where the prose contains a colon ('closed 2026-05-04T17:18Z' captures '18Z' as anchor). These also drop with the regex hardening.

Backlog metrics (computed pre-flight per PROP-030): open-issues-total=59, open-status=12, assigned-analyst=35, assigned-tinker=0, age-ge-14d=0, age-ge-30d=0, oldest-open=0.63 days. All metrics well inside green tier; no threshold fires. Backlog is in healthy steady-state; no Mode 1 escalation warranted.

previous_followup.note: This run was directive-driven and did not run the PROP Lifecycle Verification step. The next non-directive tinker run should perform that walk and update PROP statuses as appropriate.

next_actions.operator: Review PROP-069-narrative-cite-recalibration.json. If accepted, file a follow-up directive for implementation (modify monitor/scripts/audit-narrative-citations.js + monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md Step 3.6c). PROP-069 implementation estimate: ~30 minutes for someone familiar with the script; low risk (observational-only changes; no schema mutations).
next_actions.tinker: On next non-directive run, walk monitor/tinker/proposals/ to update PROP statuses per the PROP Lifecycle Verification pattern in tinker-proposals-and-fixes.md. Also consider authoring the follow-up behavioral PROP for curmudgeon citation discipline (the 1,614 curmudgeon-uncited count is the real signal post-recalibration).
